./.title: Home
./.body:
Bisma Qamar
Woman empowerment specialist · Youth advocate · UN & PMYP · PSTD

Interactive website: website/index.html — or deploy website/ to Netlify / GitHub Pages.This GitBook syncs from GitHub — edit Markdown here, push, and GitBook updates automatically.

Youth advocate · Communications & learning development · UN youth affairs (Pakistan)
Focal Person — Global Youth Affairs & United Nations Engagement, Prime Minister’s Youth Programme (PMYP). Head of conferences and global learning and Communications Lead, Learning & Development at the Pakistan Society for Training and Development (PSTD). IPS contributor and former UN National Youth Council representative (Jun–Oct 2024, New York).

At a glance









Focus
Youth inclusion, L&D, DE&I conferences, personal branding, gender-inclusive education


Base
Karachi, Pakistan · professional network across Pakistan, KSA, and UN (New York)


Experience
7+ years (marketing, welfare, e-commerce, L&D, youth diplomacy)


Education
MBA — Institute of Business Management (IoBM), Karachi (2022–2024)


Email
bisma.qamar@pstd.com.pk


Languages
English (native/bilingual) · Urdu (full professional)




Current roles (verified via LinkedIn, May 2026)



Role
Organization
Since




Focal Person — UN & Global Youth Affairs
Prime Minister’s Youth Programme (PMYP)
Nominated to Chairman, PMYP (reported Imroze Pakistan)


Head of conferences and global learning
PSTD
(current; per LinkedIn headline)


Comms Lead — Learning & Development
PSTD
Jan 2024 – present · Karachi




What I do
I connect policy dialogue, strategic communications, and youth empowerment—from corporate DE&I conferences and PSTD learning programmes to PMYP delegations at the ECOSOC Youth Forum and journalism for IPS and IDN.

Represent Pakistan’s youth in multilateral settings (Summit of the Future 2024, ECOSOC Youth Forum 2026).
Lead communications for Pakistan’s largest professional training society (PSTD, est. 1966).
Interview global leaders—including Amb. Munir Akram (Pakistan at the UN) and Dr. Felipe Paullier (UN ASG for Youth)—for international media.

Prime Minister’s Youth Programme (PMYP)
Focal Person — Global Youth Affairs & United Nations Engagement

Responsibilities (public reporting)

Advance youth-centered initiatives and capacity-building for young Pakistanis.
Coordinate strategic engagement with the United Nations and international youth forums.
Support PMYP youth delegations (e.g. ECOSOC Youth Forum 2026, New York).

united-nations.html.title: United Nations & youth diplomacy
united-nations.html.body:
United Nations & youth diplomacy
UN-related work is through youth delegate / representative pathways and PMYP focal person duties—not UN Secretariat employment.
LinkedIn UN role: Representative To UN — National Youth Council · Jun 2024 – Oct 2024 · New York, NY (4 months)

Summit of the Future & 79th UN General Assembly (2024)



Item
Date / detail




79th UNGA opened
Tuesday, 10 September 2024 (LinkedIn)


Summit of the Future theme
Multilateral Solutions for a Better Tomorrow


Youth chapter
Youth and Future Generations — IYC informal summit during high-level week


Bisma’s role
IYC representative; National Youth Council pathway (Jun–Oct 2024)



Publication: Summit of the Future: Youth and Future Generations — interview with H.E. Munir Akram, Permanent Representative of Pakistan to the UN (by Bisma Qamar)

ECOSOC Youth Forum 2026



Item
Detail




Dates
14–16 April 2026


Venue
UN Headquarters, New York


Pakistan lead
PMYP Chairman Rana Mashhood Ahmed Khan


Delegation
Bisma Qamar, Amsal Zaman, Fakhar Jabran, Fsahat ul Hassan, Rahim Sattar


Mission support
Permanent Mission of Pakistan (e.g. Counsellor Saima Saleem)



Press: TechJuice — Pakistan’s Youth Delegation at ECOSOC 2026
Side engagements: Plenary and thematic sessions; meetings with ECOSOC Youth Forum presidency, UN Youth Office, ICYF; dinner with H.E. Asim Iftikhar Ahmad, Permanent Representative of Pakistan to the UN.

Interview — UN Assistant Secretary-General for Youth (2026)



Item
Detail




Interviewee
Dr. Felipe Paullier — first ASG for Youth Affairs, Head of UN Youth Office (mandate from Dec 2023, age 32 at appointment)


Published
28 May 2026, IPS


Interviewer byline
Focal Person for UN and Global Youth Affairs, PMYP



Read: A New Youth Generation: Largest in History & a Decisive Force

PMYP — current UN coordination role
As Focal Person, Global Youth Affairs & UN Engagement (nominated to PMYP Chairman), responsibilities include international youth forums, capacity-building, and Pakistan’s representation at UN platforms (Imroze Pakistan).

Advocacy themes

Meaningful youth participation (not symbolic)  
National upskilling before international representation  
Gender-inclusive education and opportunity  
ECOSOC / HLPF as spaces for intergenerational dialogue

Bios for copy-paste
One line

Bisma Qamar is Focal Person for Global Youth Affairs and UN Engagement at Pakistan’s Prime Minister’s Youth Programme, Head of conferences and global learning and Comms Lead for L&D at PSTD, and an IPS contributor who has represented Pakistan at the UN ECOSOC Youth Forum 2026.

Short (three sentences)

Bisma Qamar is a Pakistani youth advocate and communications leader with an MBA from IoBM (2022–2024). She serves PMYP as focal person for UN and global youth affairs and leads conference and L&D communications at PSTD (since Jan 2024). She was UN National Youth Council representative in New York (Jun–Oct 2024), writes for IPS and IDN, and joined Pakistan’s ECOSOC Youth Forum 2026 delegation in New York.

Contact line

bisma.qamar@pstd.com.pk · LinkedIn: /in/bismaqamar
